quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 7, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Mixed Reaction Types Balancing
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Balancing Chemical Equations, Synthesis Reactions, Decomposition Reactions, Single Replacement, Double Replacement, Acid-Base Reactions

questions.question:
____$\text{Ca}$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ → ____$\text{Ca(OH)}_2$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $2\text{Ca} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{Ca(OH)}_2$ is balanced. Left side: 2 Ca, 4 H, 4 O; right side: 2 Ca, 4 H, 4 O.

questions.question:
___ $\text{KMnO}_4$ + ___ $\text{HCl}$ → ___ $\text{MnCl}_2$ + ___ $\text{Cl}_2$ + ___ $\text{KCl}$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $2\text{KMnO}_4 + 16\text{HCl} \rightarrow 2\text{MnCl}_2 + 5\text{Cl}_2 + 2\text{KCl} + 8\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. Left side: 2 K, 2 Mn, 8 O, 16 H, 16 Cl; right side: 2 K, 2 Mn, 8 O, 16 H, 16 Cl (from 2 MnCl₂ = 4 Cl, 5 Cl₂ = 10 Cl, 2 KCl = 2 Cl, total 16 Cl). This is the redox reaction of potassium permanganate with hydrochloric acid.

questions.question:
____$\text{K}$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$ → ____$\text{KOH}$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $4\text{K} + \text{O}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 4\text{KOH}$ is balanced. Left side: 4 K, 4 O, 4 H; right side: 4 K, 4 O, 4 H.

questions.question:
____$\text{Mg}$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ → ____$\text{Mg(OH)}_2$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $2\text{Mg} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{Mg(OH)}_2$ is balanced. Left side: 2 Mg, 4 H, 4 O; right side: 2 Mg, 4 H, 4 O.

questions.question:
____$\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$ + ____$\text{CO}_2$ → ____$\text{NaHCO}_3$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 + \text{H}_2\text{O} + \text{CO}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{NaHCO}_3$ is balanced. Left side: 2 Na, 1 C (from Na₂CO₃) + 1 C (from CO₂) = 2 C, 2 H, 6 O; right side: 2 Na, 2 C, 2 H, 6 O.

questions.question:
____$\text{Na}$ + ____$\text{S}$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ → ____$\text{Na}_2\text{SO}_4$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $2\text{Na} + \text{S} + 2\text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{SO}_4$ is balanced. Left side: 2 Na, 1 S, 4 O; right side: 2 Na, 1 S, 4 O.

questions.question:
____$\text{Ca}$ + ____$\text{S}$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ → ____$\text{CaSO}_4$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $\text{Ca} + \text{S} + 2\text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{CaSO}_4$ is balanced. Left side: 1 Ca, 1 S, 4 O; right side: 1 Ca, 1 S, 4 O.

questions.question:
____$\text{HNO}_3$ + ____$\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$ → ____$\text{NaNO}_3$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$ + ____$\text{CO}_2$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $2\text{HNO}_3 + \text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{NaNO}_3 + \text{H}_2\text{O} + \text{CO}_2$ is balanced. This is the reaction of nitric acid with sodium carbonate.

questions.question:
____$\text{HNO}_3$ + ____$\text{NaOH}$ → ____$\text{NaNO}_3$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $\text{HNO}_3 + \text{NaOH} \rightarrow \text{NaNO}_3 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$ is already balanced with coefficients 1,1,1,1. This is a simple acid-base neutralization.

questions.question:
____$\text{NaHCO}_3$ → ____$\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$ + ____$\text{CO}_2$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $2\text{NaHCO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 + \text{CO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. This is the thermal decomposition of sodium bicarbonate.

questions.question:
____$\text{Pb(NO}_3)_2$ → ____$\text{PbO}$ + ____$\text{NO}_2$ + ____$\text{O}_2$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $2\text{Pb(NO}_3)_2 \rightarrow 2\text{PbO} + 4\text{NO}_2 + \text{O}_2$ is balanced. This is the thermal decomposition of lead(II) nitrate.

questions.question:
____$\text{Al}$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$ → ____$\text{Al}_2(\text{SO}_4)_3$ + ____$\text{H}_2$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $2\text{Al} + 3\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \rightarrow \text{Al}_2(\text{SO}_4)_3 + 3\text{H}_2$ is balanced. Aluminum reacts with sulfuric acid in a single replacement reaction.

questions.question:
____$\text{Cu}$ + ____$\text{AgNO}_3$ → ____$\text{Cu(NO}_3)_2$ + ____$\text{Ag}$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $\text{Cu} + 2\text{AgNO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Cu(NO}_3)_2 + 2\text{Ag}$ is balanced. Copper displaces silver from silver nitrate.

questions.question:
____$\text{Cl}_2$ + ____$\text{NaBr}$ → ____$\text{NaCl}$ + ____$\text{Br}_2$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $\text{Cl}_2 + 2\text{NaBr} \rightarrow 2\text{NaCl} + \text{Br}_2$ is balanced. Chlorine displaces bromine from sodium bromide (halogen displacement).

questions.question:
____$\text{Pb(NO}_3)_2$ + ____$\text{KI}$ → ____$\text{PbI}_2$ + ____$\text{KNO}_3$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $\text{Pb(NO}_3)_2 + 2\text{KI} \rightarrow \text{PbI}_2 + 2\text{KNO}_3$ is balanced. This double replacement reaction produces yellow lead(II) iodide precipitate.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered balancing all types of chemical reactions! ⚖️🧪
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review decomposition reactions and reactions with multiple products.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on counting atoms carefully on both sides.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the Law of Conservation of Mass and practice balancing simpler equations first.
